items.title: ACT Labor facing monumental reckoning in wake of Berry's resignation

children.content: A government that's been in power for almost 25 years is unlikely to have an unblemished record. But while ACT Labor ministers have weathered controversy on many occasions, Yvette Berry was different.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a woman with long light hair and glasses and a man with short hair and glasses.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a woman with long light hair and glasses and a man with short hair and glasses.

items.title: 'I don't want a circus': ACT Education Minister resigns from cabinet

children.content: ACT Education Minister Yvette Berry resigns after the Integrity Commission made "serious corrupt conduct" findings against two senior public servants in her portfolio.

image.altText: Yvette Berry looking serious.

items.title: Serious corruption findings against senior ACT public servants

children.content: The commission investigated whether officials were impartial and honest in awarding an $18.8 million refurbishment contract at Campbell Primary School to Lendlease, despite a bid by local firm Manteena being deemed better and cheaper.

image.altText: Public servants and school sign

items.title: 'Bleak motorways' in Canberra's south to gain housing under draft plan

children.content: The plan is for mid-rise housing to be developed closest to transport stops and shopping centres, with high-rise development reserved for the Woden town centre and north Curtin.

image.altText: Southern transport corridor aerial

items.title: 'Her life is ruined': Hellenic Club cleared of failing to record problem gambling

children.content: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

image.altText: The exterior of a club.

items.title: AI has changed what criminals can do with your stolen Origin data

children.content: The theft of names, addresses and phone numbers may seem less serious than stolen bank details, but cyber experts say AI is changing what criminals can do with that information.

articles.title: Origin Energy confirms breach of customer data

children.content: Energy company Origin confirms there has been unauthorised access to and disclosure of some customers' data, a day after it announced it was investigating a "potential" breach.

image.altText: The Origin Energy logo on the side of a tiled building in Melbourne.

items.title: Security concerns in US over Chinese ships that visit Australia

children.content: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

image.altText: Two red and white ships break through an icy body of water.

items.title: The text warnings about a looming Liberal Party corruption scandal

children.content: Nineteen months before making the parliamentary speech that sparked a corruption inquiry into NSW Liberal figures and their relationship with a fugitive property developer, parliamentary secretary Ray Williams sent an urgent text message to his boss, premier Gladys Berejiklian.

image.altText: Gladys Berejiklian looking worried.

items.title: Conspiracy theories about the AusAlert test spreading online

children.content: From wrapping your phone in foil to secret phones, ABC NEWS Verify takes a look into some of these false claims and real concerns about AusAlert to try to answer a few questions.

image.altText: A phone displaying the AusAlert message with conspiracy theories in the background

items.title: Labor warned regional funding woes will drive One Nation support

children.content: The ALP's 50th National Conference kicked off with the rise of One Nation, local council budgets and regional voter frustrations leading negotiations.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese smiles while standing at a podium. The words "Delivering Real Change" appear on the podium and on the wall

articles.title: Glasgow was asleep and the Games weren't open as Australia got its first win

image.altText: Kelsey Cottrell high fives Australian women's pairs bowls teammate Dawn Hayman at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

articles.title: Netball agents frustrated as pay talks roll into Glasgow 2026

children.content: Messy and drawn-out pay negotiations are once again overshadowing a major tournament for the Aussie Diamonds.

image.altText: A player catches the ball with one hand on the run

children.content: The Commonwealth Games are officially opened by King Charles III as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators. Follow live.

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

items.title: Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump

children.content: Donald Trump is insisting the US deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear energy industry has one major condition — the Arab kingdom must formally recognise the State of Israel.

articles.title: Trump went to war to restrict a nuclear program, now he's backing one

children.content: Donald Trump went to war with Tehran to stop it enriching nuclear fuel. Now he's approved a deal that analysts warn could open the door for another Middle Eastern power to potentially do exactly that.

image.altText: Donald Trump points at a wall while walking with Mohammed bin Salman.

articles.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach nuclear power deal

children.content: Israeli officials and US politicians from both parties have voiced concern that a civilian nuclear project in Saudi Arabia could be used to eventually develop nuclear weapons.

image.altText: Donald Trump holding hands with Mohammed Bin Salman during a meeting in the Oval Office.

items.title: Chinese diplomats leave ALP conference after bid to eject Taiwan officials

children.content: The incident could stir fresh tensions between China and Australia in the wake of several recent high-profile disputes.

items.title: Billions in new funding for AUKUS shipyards in Adelaide

children.content: The billions of dollars in additional funding is another step towards making Australia's nuclear submarine program "a reality".

items.title: Small business insolvency rates begin to rise

children.content: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

items.title: Indonesia's baby traffickers target poor families as demand grows

children.content: Police in Indonesia are more regularly busting baby trafficking and illegal adoption rings, with officials worried poverty may be driving the practice.

items.title: Sydney's affordable house prices increase despite premium location downfalls

children.content: First home buyers are "less likely to be affected" by Sydney's housing price and unit downturn as historically affordable areas have increased, a property expert says.

items.title: Adelaide now fourth most expensive capital for buying a house

children.content: Data shows Adelaide is bucking national housing trends with median property prices rising 4.8 per cent in the June quarter.

items.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Congo

children.content: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

items.title: Violence by son-in-law left 72-year-old 'homeless and hopeless'

children.content: Reports of elder abuse in Queensland have steadily increased in recent years, with women being the most common victims. Advocates say urgent investment is needed for prevention and response.

items.title: Customers claim new mobile phone coverage maps still not accurate

children.content: Telcos were forced to publish mobile phone coverage maps to new standards last month, but some Telstra customers say they still do not reflect reality.

items.title: Police charge man over 5-year spree targeting women with AI sex posters

children.content: The 52-year-old is accused of posting fake sexualised images of women, including their full names, in public places across south-east Queensland over five years.

items.title: Queensland's 2011 floods inspire underground dance culture

children.content: Equal parts captivating and unsettling, young people from Ipswich to Japan are taking to the streets to dance "flood" style.
